title: stdlib: more missing methods (String::new, Vec::with_capacity, collect() type inference, as_str/as_ref)
eval: entry().or_insert() pattern for HashMap
description: entry(k).or_insert(v) should atomically get-or-insert a HashMap value and return a mutable ref to it (Level 0: just return current or inserted value, write back)

title: eval: resolve std::path::Type calls by stripping namespace prefix
description: std::collections::HashMap::new() should work same as HashMap::new()

title: parser: struct shorthand field init { x, y } = { x: x, y: y }
description: Desugar shorthand field init at parse time

title: stdlib: Range iterator methods (sum, count, min, max, collect, map, filter)
description: Range needs sum/count/min/max/collect/map/filter so (1..=10).sum() etc. work

title: wire crust build: parse → codegen → rustc
description: Modify src/main.rs build(). Replace the placeholder 'Hello world' stub. Read .crust file, lex+parse, run Codegen::new().emit_program(), write to temp .rs file (or --emit-rs path if flag set), invoke rustc as before. If --emit-rs flag provided, also copy the .rs to <input>.rs. Integration test in tests/integration.rs: crust build fib.crust -o /tmp/fib_test && /tmp/fib_test outputs 'fib(35) = 9227465'. crust build point.crust -o /tmp/point_test && /tmp/point_test outputs 'Distance: 5'.

title: codegen: emit valid .rs from AST (Level 0)
description: Create src/codegen.rs. Codegen struct with emit_program(items) -> String. Walk AST and emit Rust source. Level 0 transforms: add #[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)] before every struct/enum. Expand Expr::Macro back to macro_rules syntax (println! vec! format! etc). Emit explicit .clone() on every ident use in a position that could be a move (conservative: clone everything that isn't Copy). Wrap output in standard prelude (use std::collections::HashMap;). emit_expr, emit_stmt, emit_item, emit_fn, emit_struct, emit_impl, emit_block - each returns String. Indentation via depth parameter. Tests: emit fib.crust source, compile it with rustc, run it, check output == 'fib(35) = 9227465'.

title: REPL: interactive loop with persistent env (rustyline)
description: Add rustyline = "14" to Cargo.toml. Create src/repl.rs. Repl struct holds persistent Interpreter. Loop: rustyline prompt '>> ', on input try lex+parse as stmt; if parse fails as stmt try as expr; eval in persistent env. Print non-unit results automatically (like Python REPL). :quit/:exit exits. :type <expr> prints inferred type name. On error: print error, keep going (don't kill the session). Handle incomplete input (open braces) by appending '... ' continuation prompt. Wire to main.rs: Some("repl") => repl::run(). Manual test: launch crust repl, type let x = 5, then x+3, expect 8.

title: wire crust run: parse + eval real .crust files
description: Modify src/main.rs run(). Read file from args[0], error if missing/unreadable. Lexer::new(source).tokenize(), Parser::new(tokens).parse_program(), Interpreter::new().run(program). Print runtime errors to stderr with line info. Integration test: run hello.crust → 3 greeting lines + count line. run fib.crust → 'fib(35) = 9227465'. run point.crust → 'Distance: 5'. Add these as #[test] in tests/integration.rs using std::process::Command to invoke the binary.

title: stdlib: built-in macros + methods for Level 0
description: Create src/stdlib.rs. Expand Expr::Macro at eval time. println!(fmt, args...) - basic {} interpolation with Display. format!(fmt, args...) -> Str. vec![a,b,c] -> Vec. assert_eq!(a,b) panics with message on mismatch. assert!(cond). panic!(msg). Built-in methods dispatched in eval_method_call when receiver is a known type: Vec: push pop len iter contains, String/Str: len contains to_uppercase to_lowercase trim split, Option: unwrap unwrap_or is_some is_none, Result: unwrap expect is_ok is_err. to_string() on any value. parse::<i64>() and parse::<f64>() on Str. Tests: println! captures output, vec! builds correct Vec, assert_eq! panics on mismatch.

title: evaluator: expression + statement eval (Level 0)
description: Create src/eval.rs. Interpreter struct holds global Env + fn/struct/impl registries. eval_expr(expr, env) -> Result<Value,CrustError>. eval_stmt(stmt, env) -> Result<Option<Value>,CrustError>. eval_block(block, env) -> Result<Value,CrustError> (tail expr is return value). Level 0 rules: every value is cloned on use, no move errors, no borrow errors. Implement: all binary/unary ops, let bindings, if/else, while, for-in over Vec/Range, loop+break, match (literal and enum patterns), fn definitions registered in global scope, fn calls with new child env, closures capturing env via Rc<RefCell>, struct instantiation, field access, method dispatch via impl registry. Use ControlFlow enum (Return(Value) Break(Option<Value>) Continue) for non-local exits, propagate through eval_block. Tests: eval fib(10)==55, eval Point::distance, for-in over vec.

title: runtime: Value enum + Environment scoping
description: Create src/value.rs and src/env.rs. Value enum: Int(i64) Float(f64) Bool(bool) Str(String) Char(char) Unit Vec(Vec<Value>) HashMap(HashMap<String,Value>) Struct{type_name,fields:HashMap<String,Value>} Enum{type_name,variant,inner:Option<Box<Value>>} Fn(FnVal) Closure{params,body,captured:Rc<RefCell<Env>>} Tuple(Vec<Value>) Range(i64,i64,bool). impl Display for Value (used by println!). Env struct: vars:HashMap<String,Value>, parent:Option<Rc<RefCell<Env>>>. Methods: get(name), set(name,value), set_local(name,value), child() -> new child scope. Tests: nested scopes, shadowing, get from parent.

title: parser: recursive descent for Rust subset
description: Create src/parser.rs. Parser struct holds Vec<Token> + cursor. parse_program() -> Vec<Item>. parse_item() handles fn/struct/enum/impl/use. parse_expr() with Pratt-style precedence climbing for binary ops. parse_stmt() handles let/expr. Handle all constructs in the three example files: vec literal macro, for-in, fn with return type, struct def, impl block, method calls, format! macro, if/else, recursive calls. Macro invocations (println! vec! format! assert_eq! panic!) stored as Expr::Macro { name, raw_args }. Tests: parse each of hello.crust fib.crust point.crust without error, assert top-level item count.

title: AST: define node types for Rust subset
description: Create src/ast.rs. Enums: Lit (Int/Float/Bool/Str/Char), UnOp (Neg/Not/Deref/Ref/RefMut), BinOp (arithmetic, comparison, logic, ranges), Expr (Lit/Ident/Unary/Binary/Assign/OpAssign/Call/MethodCall/Field/Index/If/Match/Block/Closure/StructLit/Array/Tuple/Return/Break/Continue/Macro/Cast/Path), Block { stmts, tail }, Stmt (Let/ExprSemi/Semi/Item), Pat (Ident/Tuple/Struct/Wild), Ty (Named/Ref/Slice/Tuple/Never), Item (Fn/Struct/Enum/Impl/Use/Const), FnDef/StructDef/EnumDef/ImplDef. No logic — pure data. Derive Debug+Clone on everything.

title: lexer: tokenize Rust subset
description: Create src/lexer.rs. TokenKind enum covers: integer/float/bool/string/char literals, all keywords (fn let mut if else while for in loop break continue return struct enum impl trait use pub self true false match const), all operators and punctuation, Ident. Lexer struct with tokenize() -> Vec<Token>. Attach line/col to each Token. Handle // line comments and /* block */ comments. Tests: tokenize hello.crust, fib.crust, point.crust.

title: error types: add CrustError with thiserror
description: Create src/error.rs. Add thiserror to Cargo.toml. Define CrustError enum: ParseError { msg, line, col }, RuntimeError { msg }, IoError. Implement Display. Unit test that each variant formats correctly.

title: crust v0.2: working interpreter + REPL + Level 0 codegen
description: Build the full pipeline: lexer → parser → tree-walk interpreter (Level 0, no borrow checker) → REPL → code generator. After this, crust run, crust repl, and crust build all do real work on the three example files.
